title: shani-update — The Shani OS Update Manager
excerpt: shani-update is the user-facing update manager for Shani OS. It runs automatically via a systemd user timer and at login, detects boot failures, checks for staged updates awaiting a reboot, identifies newly deployed slots, and checks for new OS images — all with GUI dialogs, automatic terminal detection, and a deferred-reminder system.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: Adding LUKS2 Encryption After Installation on Shani OS
excerpt: Did not enable LUKS2 encryption during installation? This guide walks through adding full-disk encryption to an existing unencrypted Shani OS installation — backing up data, repartitioning with LUKS2, restoring, and enrolling TPM2 for passwordless unlock.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: Shani OS vs Fedora Silverblue, Bazzite, SteamOS, and NixOS — An Honest Comparison
excerpt: An honest technical comparison of immutable Linux distributions. Where Shani OS wins, where others genuinely win, and how to pick the right one for your situation. Btrfs send/receive vs OSTree vs OCI images, gaming focus, enterprise needs, and declarative vs imperative philosophy.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: Running Windows Apps on Shani OS — Wine, Bottles, and When to Use a VM
excerpt: You do not need Windows to run Windows software on Shani OS. Bottles (pre-installed on the KDE Plasma edition) manages Wine environments for productivity tools, creative software, and legacy apps. Here is how to get any Windows app running, and when a VM is the better answer.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: Apptainer (Singularity) on Shani OS — HPC Containers for Workstation and Cluster
excerpt: Apptainer is the HPC standard for portable, reproducible containers — pre-installed on Shani OS, no root required, runs the same SIF image on your workstation and on any SLURM/PBS cluster. Build once, run anywhere, with full NVIDIA and AMD ROCm GPU support.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: GPU Compute on Shani OS — NVIDIA CUDA, AMD ROCm, and Intel oneAPI
excerpt: How to run GPU compute workloads on Shani OS — setting up AMD ROCm with PyTorch and TensorFlow via Distrobox, NVIDIA CUDA via Distrobox, Intel oneAPI, HIP for cross-vendor portability, and Nix for pure ML environments. Works for ML, simulation, data science, and scientific computing.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: LXC and LXD on Shani OS — Full System Containers
excerpt: LXC and LXD run full Linux system containers on Shani OS — complete OS environments sharing the host kernel, stored in dedicated @lxc and @lxd Btrfs subvolumes that survive every OS update. Lighter than VMs, more complete than Distrobox.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: systemd-nspawn and machinectl on Shani OS — Lightweight System Containers
excerpt: systemd-nspawn is the lightest way to run a full Linux system environment on Shani OS — no image management, no daemon, no setup. Containers boot in under a second, live in the @machines Btrfs subvolume, and survive every OS update untouched.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: System Configuration on Shani OS — /etc, Services, and What Persists
excerpt: The /etc OverlayFS on Shani OS means all your configuration changes persist normally across every OS update and rollback. This guide covers editing config files, managing systemd services, enabling services, kernel parameters, and inspecting what you have customised.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: Audio on Shani OS — PipeWire, WirePlumber, JACK, and Professional Audio
excerpt: Shani OS uses PipeWire 1.4.x as its audio stack — simultaneously compatible with ALSA, PulseAudio, and JACK. This guide covers everyday audio management, Bluetooth codec selection, fixing common issues, and setting up low-latency professional audio workflows.
author: Shrinivas Vishnu Kumbhar
author_bio: Shrinivas is a cloud expert, DevOps engineer, and creator of Shani OS.

title: KDE Connect and GSConnect on Shani OS — Your Phone and Desktop, Together
excerpt: KDE Connect (KDE edition) and GSConnect (GNOME edition) link your Android phone to your Shani OS desktop — notifications mirrored, files shared, clipboard synced, remote input, SMS from desktop, and media control. Firewall rules are pre-configured.

title: Indian Language Support on Shani OS — Devanagari, Tamil, Telugu, and More

title: AppImage on Shani OS — Portable Apps with Gear Lever
excerpt: AppImages are self-contained portable executables that need no installation and work on any Linux system. Gear Lever (pre-installed) integrates them into your desktop launcher, manages updates, and keeps them in persistent storage that survives OS updates.
